text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST
RESTAGING EXAMINATION — SECOND INTERVAL ASSESSMENT ON SYSTEMIC THERAPY

CLINICAL INDICATION:
Known metastatic clear cell renal cell carcinoma with sarcomatoid differentiation. History of cytoreductive right radical nephrectomy with retroperitoneal lymph node dissection and IVC thrombectomy. On systemic therapy with Yervoy and Opdivo (ipi/nivo combination), now in Opdivo maintenance phase following completion of four-cycle ipi induction. Prior restaging at approximately 3 months demonstrated partial response with meaningful reduction in bilateral pulmonary metastases. Current examination performed for second interval response assessment at approximately 6 months of combined/maintenance immunotherapy. Comparison made to most recent prior CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (approximately 3 months prior, at first restaging) and to the pre-therapy baseline CT (approximately 6 weeks post-nephrectomy).

TECHNIQUE:
Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis was performed following the uneventful administration of approximately 100 mL of low-osmolar intravenous iodinated contrast (isovuersol 320). Acquisitions performed in the portal venous phase for the abdomen and pelvis. Chest acquired in the venous phase. No oral contrast administered per departmental oncology protocol. Coronal and sagittal multiplanar reformations reviewed. Lung window, mediastinal window, and bone window reconstructions reviewed.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and airways: The trachea and central airways are patent without endobronchial lesions. No central airway narrowing.

Right lower lobe pulmonary metastasis (index/target lesion, previously 1.3 cm at first restaging): This previously well-defined subpleural nodule in the right lower lobe has increased in size, now measuring approximately 2.4 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 1.3 cm at first restaging, previously 2.1 cm at baseline pre-therapy). The lesion remains rounded with well-circumscribed margins and demonstrates soft tissue attenuation without internal cavitation or calcification. There is no evidence of satellite nodularity or pleural invasion. Compared to the prior restaging examination, this lesion has roughly doubled in size and now approaches its original pre-treatment baseline dimension. This growth pattern raises concern for loss of immunotherapy response at this site.

Right upper lobe pulmonary nodule (target lesion): A rounded right upper lobe nodule is identified, measuring approximately 0.6 cm in greatest axial dimension (previously 0.7 cm at first restaging, previously 1.2 cm at baseline). This lesion appears essentially stable in size and morphology compared to the prior restaging examination. It remains smaller than baseline, consistent with persistent partial response at this site.

Left lower lobe pulmonary nodule (target lesion): A left lower lobe nodule measuring approximately 0.5 cm in greatest axial dimension (stable compared to 0.5 cm at first restaging, previously 0.9 cm at baseline). This lesion appears unchanged since first restaging and continues to reflect partial regression from the pre-therapy baseline.

Left upper lobe pulmonary nodule (target lesion): A small left upper lobe nodule measuring approximately 0.4 cm in greatest axial dimension (stable compared to 0.4 cm at first restaging, previously 0.7 cm at baseline). Stable and not appreciably changed from the prior examination. Continued partial regression from baseline.

Additional pulmonary findings: No new pulmonary nodules identified above the threshold of reliable detection. No new lung mass lesions. No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. No pulmonary edema or consolidation. No areas of ground-glass opacity suspicious for immune-mediated pneumonitis.

Mediastinum and hila: The mediastinum is not widened. No mediastinal or hilar lymphadenopathy. The heart and pericardium appear unremarkable. No pericardial effusion. The thoracic aorta is normal in caliber without aneurysmal dilatation. No suspicious mediastinal soft tissue mass.

Chest wall and pleura: Bilateral pleural surfaces are smooth and unremarkable. No pleural thickening. No chest wall involvement by tumor.

Skeleton (thoracic): No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesions. No pathologic fracture. No suspicious bone lesion identified within the thoracic skeleton on this non-dedicated evaluation.

---

ABDOMEN:

Liver: The liver parenchyma is homogeneous in attenuation without focal hepatic lesions identified. No hypo- or hyperattenuating nodules. No biliary ductal dilatation. The hepatic vasculature appears patent. No perihepatic free fluid. The gallbladder is present without gallstones or wall thickening.

Spleen: Normal in size and homogeneous in attenuation. No splenic lesions.

Pancreas: Unremarkable. The pancreatic duct is not dilated. No pancreatic mass lesion.

Adrenal glands:
— Left adrenal: Normal morphology. No mass lesion.
— Right adrenal: A new nodular soft tissue density is identified in the right adrenal gland, measuring approximately 1.3 cm in greatest dimension, with smooth margins and attenuation characteristics that do not fulfill CT washout criteria for a lipid-rich adenoma. This lesion was not present on the baseline pre-therapy CT or on the prior first restaging examination. Given the clinical context of known metastatic clear cell RCC, this new right adrenal nodule is suspicious for an adrenal metastasis. Biochemical and tissue characterization recommended.

Kidneys:
— Right: The right kidney has been surgically removed (cytoreductive nephrectomy). The right renal fossa demonstrates expected post-operative soft tissue fibrosis and fatty stranding without evidence of local tumor recurrence. The surgical bed appears intact. The inferior vena cava is patent with no intraluminal filling defect or thrombus (site of prior IVC thrombectomy now cleared); the IVC wall at the prior thrombus site shows mild thickening consistent with post-surgical change.
— Left: The left kidney appears normal in size with preserved parenchymal thickness and corticomedullary differentiation. No hydronephrosis. No obstructing calculi. No left renal mass lesion. The left renal artery and vein are patent.

Retroperitoneum: Post-operative changes in the right retroperitoneum are again noted, consistent with the prior RPLND. The right retroperitoneal soft tissue fibrosis appears stable from the prior examination. No new or recurrent retroperitoneal lymphadenopathy identified. The aortocaval, right para-aortic, and right common iliac nodal regions are clear of suspicious lymphadenopathy. The inferior vena cava is patent throughout its visualized course.

Bowel: The small bowel and large bowel appear unremarkable in caliber and wall thickness. No bowel obstruction. No abnormal mural enhancement or wall thickening. No pneumatosis or free intraperitoneal air. No free intraperitoneal fluid.

Abdominal vasculature: The abdominal aorta is normal in caliber. Celiac, SMA, and SMA appear patent. No vascular thrombosis.

---

PELVIS:

Bladder: Normal wall thickness and contour. No filling defect. No perilesional fat stranding.

Female reproductive organs: Uterus is present and unremarkable in size. Both ovaries are not separately identified, which is consistent with post-menopausal status. No adnexal mass. No pelvic free fluid.

Pelvic lymph nodes: No pelvic lymphadenopathy. The external and internal iliac nodal chains are clear.

Musculoskeletal (pelvic): No lytic or sclerotic osseous lesion within the visualized pelvis, sacrum, or proximal femora. No pathologic fracture.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. MIXED RESPONSE on second interval restaging following approximately 6 months of immunotherapy (ipi/nivo, currently nivo maintenance). Specifically:

   a. The right lower lobe pulmonary nodule — previously the dominant target lesion — has markedly enlarged, now measuring 2.4 cm (from a nadir of 1.3 cm at first restaging), approaching its original pre-treatment size. This isolated growth at one site, in the setting of otherwise stable or improved disease elsewhere, raises concern for oligoprogression or site-specific loss of immunotherapy response.

   b. The remaining three pulmonary metastases (right upper lobe, left lower lobe, and left upper lobe) remain stable in size compared to the first restaging, all still considerably smaller than the pre-therapy baseline, consistent with maintained partial response at these sites.

2. NEW right adrenal nodule measuring approximately 1.3 cm, not present on baseline or prior restaging imaging. The attenuation characteristics are not consistent with a benign lipid-rich adenoma in this clinical setting. Given the known history of metastatic ccRCC, adrenal metastasis is the leading diagnostic consideration. Tissue sampling is warranted for confirmation prior to directing local therapy.

3. No new hepatic, osseous, or additional visceral metastases identified on this examination.

4. No retroperitoneal nodal recurrence in the post-surgical bed. Stable post-operative changes at the right nephrectomy/RPLND site. IVC remains patent without thrombus recurrence.

5. No radiographic evidence of immune-mediated pneumonitis, hepatitis, or colitis.

6. Continued attention to the right lower lobe lesion and the right adrenal nodule is recommended given the discordant response pattern. Clinical correlation with oncologic management team is advised.
